calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-04-2024 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-22-2024 Continued To Apr-17-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-27-2023 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer, which appears to be unopposed, is sustained. Plaintiff's complaint fails to state a cause of action against the City. (See Code Civ. Proc., Section Code 430.10, subd. (e).) As a separate and independent grounds for sustaining the demurrer, Plaintiff's complaint is uncertain and unintelligible to the point the City cannot reasonably respond. (See Code Civ. Proc., Section Code 430.10, subd. (f).) It is not apparent from the complaint that Plaintiff can amend to state a cause of action against the City, particularly in light of the Government Claims Act, and Plaintiff has not made any showing that curative amendment is reasonably possible. (See Jenkins v. JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A. (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 497, 506-507, as modified (June 12, 2013) [plaintiff bears burden to show curative amendment reasonably possible.]) Indeed, Plaintiff filed a "Pleading to Withdraw," indicating he did not wish to proceed with this action. Thus, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2023 Continued To Feb-14-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
